SEARCH

Uses of Record Class
common.dto.SubscriptionKey

Packages that use SubscriptionKey
Package
Description
client.net
Client networking: the persistent TCP connection to the server and the event bus that delivers server-pushed real-time updates.
client.view
Client JavaFX controllers and view helpers — one controller per FXML screen (login, dashboard, reservations, gate, reports, approval queue, notifications, registration, …) plus shared UI utilities such as BaseController and Widgets.
server.net
Server networking: the OCSF-style TCP server that accepts client connections, tracks each ClientSession, routes incoming ClientRequests to the domain layer via RequestRouter, and reports lifecycle events through the ServerListener callback interface.
server.subscription
Real-time push registry: SubscriptionRegistry maps subscription keys (e.g.

  • Uses of SubscriptionKey in client.net

    Fields in client.net declared as SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final SubscriptionKey
    EventBus.Subscription.key
    The key this subscription is registered against.
    Fields in client.net with type parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final ConcurrentHashMap<SubscriptionKey,List<Consumer<ServerEvent>>>
    EventBus.subscribers
    Subscriber callbacks keyed by subscription key.
    Methods in client.net that return SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    SubscriptionKey
    EventBus.Subscription.getKey()
    Returns the resource this subscription is registered against; used by controllers to issue a matching UNSUBSCRIBE request on the wire when they detach locally.
    Methods in client.net with parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    (package private) void
    EventBus.removeSubscriber(SubscriptionKey key, Consumer<ServerEvent> cb)
    Removes cb from the subscriber list for key.
    EventBus.Subscription
    EventBus.subscribe(SubscriptionKey key, Consumer<ServerEvent> cb)
    Registers cb to be invoked on the FX thread whenever the server pushes an event for key.
    Constructors in client.net with parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
    private
    Subscription(SubscriptionKey key, Consumer<ServerEvent> cb)
    Creates a subscription handle.

    Fields in client.view declared as SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private SubscriptionKey
    MainShellController.notifKey
    The key MainShellController.notifSub is registered against, retained so logout can UNSUBSCRIBE it.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final Set<SubscriptionKey>
    ClientSession.subscriptions
    The push subscriptions this connection currently holds.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Set<SubscriptionKey>
    ClientSession.subscriptions()
    Read-only snapshot of every subscription this session currently holds.
    Methods in server.net with parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    ClientSession.subscribe(SubscriptionKey key)
    Records that this client now wants change events for key.
    void
    ClientSession.unsubscribe(SubscriptionKey key)
    Removes a previous subscription.

  • Uses of SubscriptionKey in server.subscription

    Fields in server.subscription with type parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final ConcurrentHashMap<SubscriptionKey,Set<ClientSession>>
    SubscriptionRegistry.map
    Subscription key → the set of sessions subscribed to it.
    Methods in server.subscription with parameters of type SubscriptionKey
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    SubscriptionRegistry.publish(SubscriptionKey key, ServerEvent event)
    Fans event out to every session subscribed to key.
    void
    SubscriptionRegistry.register(ClientSession session, SubscriptionKey key)
    Records that session wants events for key, both in the shared registry bucket and on the session itself (so disconnect cleanup can find every key the session held).
    void
    SubscriptionRegistry.unregister(ClientSession session, SubscriptionKey key)
    Reverses a previous SubscriptionRegistry.register(server.net.ClientSession, common.dto.SubscriptionKey).
